modules.title: Experimental Design & Phenology
modules.description: Learn spatial control, augmented block layouts, and data cleanup in field phenotyping trials.
readList: Walsh & Lynch (1998) Genetics and Analysis of Quantitative Traits - Chapters 1-3
Scribbr Academic Guide: Introduction to Experimental Design (https://www.scribbr.com/methodology/experimental-design/)

notebookCells.content:
# Pre-Exercise 1: Augmented Block Design Analysis
This exercise will teach you how to analyze an augmented randomized block design, common in breeding programs where seeds for new lines are highly limited and cannot be duplicated.

# Import necessary R libraries
library(survival)
library(lme4)
# Create mockup dataset for 10 checks and 40 test entries
set.seed(42)
checks_data <- data.frame(
  Block = rep(1:4, each=3),
  Entry = rep(paste("Check", 1:3), 4),
  Yield = rnorm(12, mean=45, sd=4.2),
  IsCheck = TRUE
)
test_data <- data.frame(
  Block = rep(1:4, each=10),
  Entry = paste("Entry", 1:40),
  Yield = rnorm(40, mean=42, sd=5.5) + rep(c(-2, 1, 3, -1), each=10),
  IsCheck = FALSE
)
dataset <- rbind(checks_data, test_data)
head(dataset, n=12)

# Run simple mixed model to compute Best Linear Unbiased Estimates (BLUEs)
model = lmer(Yield ~ Entry + (1|Block), data=dataset)
cat("Augmented model convergence successful! \nBLUEs generated for 40 test entrants.")

modules.title: Quantitative Genetics & Association Mapping
modules.description: Link genomic variants to phenotypes. Understand genetic correlations, kinship calculations, and linear models.
readList: Yu et al. (2006) A unified mixed-model method for association mapping (https://www.nature.com/articles/ng1702)
Price et al. (2006) Principal components analysis corrects for stratification in GWAS (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/16862161/)
NIH PMC Review: Quantitative Genetics in the Era of High-Throughput Phenotyping & Genotyping (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6431252/)

notebookCells.content:
# Pre-Exercise 2: Calculating Genomic Relationship Matrix (Kinship)
In this exercise, we compute a baseline realized kinship matrix (A-matrix) using simulated SNP markers.

# Step 1: Simulate genotyping matrix (SNP matrix: 0, 1, 2 representing reference alleles)
set.seed(123)
n_samples <- 50
n_markers <- 100
M <- matrix(sample(0:2, n_samples * n_markers, replace=TRUE), nrow=n_samples)
rownames(M) <- paste("Sample", 1:n_samples)
colnames(M) <- paste("SNP_Marker", 1:n_markers)

# Step 2: Display Genotype matrix subset
M[1:6, 1:8]

# Compute kinship matrix G using VanRaden framework (2008)
p <- colMeans(M) / 2
P <- matrix(rep(2 * p, each = n_samples), nrow = n_samples)
Z <- (M - P)
denominator <- 2 * sum(p * (1 - p))
G <- (Z %*% t(Z)) / denominator
diag(G)[1:5] # Display diagonal entries (self-relatedness)

modules.title: Practical GWAS Pipelines
modules.description: Execute end-to-end GWAS mapping on breeder datasets. Interpret Manhattan plots and identify significant candidate genes.
readList: Lipka et al. (2012) GAPIT: genome association and prediction integrated tool (https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22796954/)
Zhou & Stephens (2012) GEMMA: Genome-wide efficient mixed-model association (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3384594/)
NIH PMC Tutorial: A Guide to Conducting Genome-Wide Association Studies (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6822637/)

modules.title: Machine Learning in Agriculture
modules.description: Deploy Random Forests, gradient boosting (XGBoost), and deep learning to model agronomic features and complex GxE interactions.
readList: Howard et al. (2014) Robustness of ML for genomic prediction (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3931566/)
Crossa et al. (2017) Genomic selection in plant breeding: methods, models, and perspectives (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5523933/)
Frontiers in Plant Science: Machine Learning in Plant Breeding (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8242082/)

# Exercise 4: Random Forest for Breeding Value Classification
Let's use random forest regression to predict grain yields based on dynamic crop reflectance traits.

modules.title: Genomic Selection & Breeding value Prediction
modules.description: Learn rrBLUP, GBLUP, and genomic ranking metrics. Predict performance of unphenotyped segregating offspring to accelerate selection cycles.
readList: Meuwissen et al. (2001) Prediction of total genetic value using dense marker maps (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC1461589/)
Endelman, J.B. (2011) Ridge regression with R package rrBLUP (https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/abs/10.3835/plantgenome2011.08.0024)
Frontiers Review: Genomic Selection in Plant Breeding (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5829110/)

# Exercise 5: Single and Multi-environment Genomic Selection
Calculate genomic breeding values (GEBVs) and practice five-fold cross-validation.

workflows.name: GWAS EMMAX & GEMMA Pipe
workflows.description: Calculates principal components, builds relationship/kinship matrices, runs mixed linear models, and formats significant locus candidates into Manhattan plots.

workflows.name: Genomic Selection rrBLUP Engine
workflows.description: Calibrates Ridge Regression Best Linear Unbiased Prediction model across multiple environmental subsets and computes validation prediction accuracies.

workflows.name: Population Structure (Admixture + PCA)
workflows.description: Runs linkage disequilibrium (LD) pruning, spawns fastSTRUCTURE-like Ancestry Matrix (K=2 to K=6), and maps primary clusters into PCA space.

helpTickets.description: I am trying to run GEMMA using my Durum Wheat crop matrix but keep getting a singular matrix warning when constructing kinship. I have 198 samples and 45k SNPs. Is this caused by high sample missingness (some test rows are missing >20% values)?

comments.text: Hi Amina, yes! High missingness causes issues inside GEMMA's relationship matrix calculation. Please check your data upload QC dashboard, apply the recommended missingness filter (threshold to <10%), re-download the cleaned VCF export, and retry. It should solve the matrix singularities.

helpTickets.description: I cannot get Docker to spin up on my Windows machine. 'docker: command not found' inside raw PowerShell. I downloaded Docker Desktop. Do I need to enable WSL2 kernel integration manually?

comments.text: Hello John, make sure you start Docker Desktop first. Also inside Docker Desktop Settings, go to General and check 'Use the WSL 2 based engine'. Then reboot your powershell terminal.

helpTickets.description: How strictly should phenotype column naming match the expected template for the Genomic Selection runner? The runner expects 'Env' and 'Yield_kg_ha' but my column is named 'Yield_KG_HECTARE' with some missing NA cells.

comments.text: Hi Sarah! Yes, the companion model parses column headers literally. I added a mapping resolver so you can rename it during dataset configuration, or you can use the interactive fix button inside our validation screen to auto-format it.

showcases.title: Identifying Heat Tolerance Genomic Regions in Sahelian Chickpeas
showcases.description: Conducted GWAS mapping across 500 dryland check lines. Discovered three significant SNP nodes on Chromosome 4 associated with robust biomass production under ambient heat conditions.
